commandFailureBehavior: command failure remains blocking for the smoke command itself
timingFailureBehavior: timing over target remains advisory and does not fail CI

laneDefinitions.classification: future analyzer setup/preflight; not a Request IR Candidate and not every request critical path unless the pack is missing or stale

laneDefinitions.laneRole: deterministic user request to instruction pack frontend path
laneDefinitions.classification: core deterministic frontend path after a Request IR Candidate exists

laneDefinitions.laneRole: report-only hook gateway readiness and advisory context preparation
laneDefinitions.classification: activation readiness reporting only; not hook script implementation or blocking behavior

laneDefinitions.laneRole: advisory backend, post-check, graph delta, and review reporting
laneDefinitions.classification: advisory/post-check/reporting path; useful in all-steps smoke but not the core request-to-pack lane

futureCommandInclusionRule.coreCriticalLaneRule: Only deterministic commands required to move from validated Request IR Candidate toward execution Instruction Pack belong in core-critical-lane.
futureCommandInclusionRule.preflightRule: Prompt-pack setup, hook health, inventory, report-only, and calibration-only commands should use non-critical lanes unless explicitly promoted by a later decision.
futureCommandInclusionRule.allStepsSmokeRule: The all-steps smoke may continue to include additional deterministic commands as an advisory snapshot, but lane totals preserve the meaning of the core budget.
futureCommandInclusionRule.notAutomatic: New report-only commands must not automatically enter core-critical-lane.

forbiddenUse: treat lane budget overage as CI failure
turn timing over target into a required check
drop existing all-steps smoke coverage without an explicit decision
promote report-only commands into the core-critical lane automatically
enable strict or guided hook blocking
nonEnforcementBoundary: This artifact previews DevView runtime smoke lane policy only. It does not enforce runtime budgets, fail CI on timing, reject diffs, enable strict or guided hook blocking, mutate graph-source, apply graph deltas, approve work, record human decisions, satisfy runtime Evidence, prove equivalence, enforce scope, or configure CI.
